name: Electric toothbrushes
summary: Rechargeable electric toothbrushes — subscription-heavy consumable category.

evidence.claim: ADA Seal of Acceptance is the credible third-party endorsement for toothbrushes; Oral-B and Philips Sonicare have most models certified.
Replacement brush heads at manufacturer prices run $5-$15 each; 3-month replacement cycle means $20-$60/year per user — can exceed the brush handle's annual depreciation.

confabulationPatterns.reality: Cochrane 2014 meta-analysis found electric toothbrushes reduce plaque ~11% and gingivitis ~6% over 1-3 months vs manual. '7x' figures are marketing.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Is the '7x' claim sourced to independent research?

confabulationPatterns.reality: Generic claim. Look for ADA Seal of Acceptance or specific endorsements from accredited dental associations (E1).
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Is the 'dentist recommended' claim specific (ADA Seal) or generic?

counterfeitSignals.signal: Brush head 4-pack at 70% off manufacturer price from unauthorized seller
counterfeitSignals.action: flag — counterfeit brush heads have been reported to cause gum irritation due to inconsistent bristle quality

compatibilityQuestions: Do you want in-app coaching, or a simple timer? Sonicare/Oral-B app features vary.
Braces or sensitive gums? Some models have gentler modes.

repairability.notes: Battery typically not user-replaceable; rechargeable handles are replace-don't-repair.
